title: Srebrenica20 / Genocide in Eight Acts
author_or_org: International Residual Mechanism for Criminal Tribunals
strengths: Tribunal-grounded synthesis that states the enclave population at the fall and the minimum killing scale, and explicitly attributes the killings to Bosnian Serb army and police forces.
limitations: A summary page rather than a raw dataset; uses rounded phrases such as 'more than 7,000' and does not produce a civilian/military split.
notes: States that about 42,000 inhabitants were in the enclave at the takeover, of whom roughly 36,000 were refugees, and that more than 7,000 Bosnian Muslim men and boys were executed.

title: The Fall of Srebrenica and the Failure of UN Peacekeeping
geographic_scope: Srebrenica enclave, Potočari, and the escape corridor
strengths: Early investigative report with direct witness and survivor detail on enclave population, Potočari separations, and the mixed civilian/armed composition of the column.
limitations: Produced before later forensic identification work and therefore stronger on event structure and classification ambiguity than on a final death total.
notes: Reports an enclave population of about 40,000; a column of roughly 12,000-15,000 people; 3,000-4,000 armed among them; and that the majority were civilians.

title: DNA Results Reveal 6481 Srebrenica Victims
strengths: Forensic program source linking DNA-led identifications to an estimate of the missing from the fall of Srebrenica.
limitations: Press release rather than a full methodological paper; 'around 8,100 missing' is not a formal civilian/military split.
notes: States that DNA evidence had identified 6,481 victims and that ICMP supported an estimate of around 8,100 individuals missing from the fall of Srebrenica.

title: List of the victims of the Srebrenica genocide
strengths: Named-victim list useful as an upper-end memorialized count of those killed in the Srebrenica genocide.
limitations: Memorial compilation rather than a status-coded analytical dataset; should not be treated as a clean civilian-only count.
notes: The page states that 8,372 victims, mostly men and boys, are listed.
